prompt:
Using the pharmaversesdtm CDISC Pilot Study LB domain and a completed
ADSL, derive an ADaM BDS Findings dataset (ADLB) for laboratory values.

Input data must be loaded directly from the pharmaverse R packages:

```r
library(pharmaversesdtm)
library(pharmaverseadam)
lb   <- pharmaversesdtm::lb
adsl <- pharmaverseadam::adsl  # 306 subjects
```

Do not generate synthetic or simulated data. Scripts that construct
LB or ADSL records from scratch will fail the benchmark.

Parameter scope: include all LBTESTCD values present in
pharmaversesdtm::lb. PARAMCD and PARAM should be mapped from a
spec-driven lookup table, not hardcoded case_when() chains.

Baseline definition: the last non-missing record on or before TRTSDT
per subject per parameter (ADT <= TRTSDT, !is.na(AVAL)).

ANL01FL: the last on-treatment non-missing non-DTYPE record per subject
per parameter per analysis visit (ADT >= TRTSDT, !is.na(AVAL),
is.na(DTYPE)).

Normal range indicators (ANRIND): "LOW" / "NORMAL" / "HIGH" based on
ANRLO and ANRHI sourced from the LB domain (LBSTNRLO, LBSTNRHI).

Include at minimum: STUDYID, USUBJID, PARAMCD, PARAM, PARAMN, AVISIT,
AVISITN, ADT, ADTF, ADY, AVAL, AVALC, AVALU, ABLFL, BASE, BASEC,
CHG, PCHG, ANRLO, ANRHI, ANRIND, BNRIND, ANL01FL, TRTSDT, TRTEDT,
TRT01P, TRT01PN, TRT01A, TRT01AN, SAFFL, ITTFL.
expected_output:
Executable R code producing an ADLB dataset with one record per subject
per parameter per analysis visit that matches pharmaverseadam::adlb on
key variables (ADT, AVAL, BASE, CHG, ABLFL, ANRIND per USUBJID /
PARAMCD / AVISIT).
